decision_target: Decide whether a fixed controller-flow loop, per-exec tags, awaited promises, a current runtime resource, and explicit ports cover bounded issue queue work without start, spawn, WorkerRegistry, or a public pool.
source_of_truth: The v2 eight-case queue probe, modeled public graph surface, fixture-excluding surface probe, exact repository-root replay, prior DKR-4 validator rejection, and DKR-2 candidate contract.
read_method: Run bash .okra/runs/issue-triage-session-20260715/workers/dkr-4-v2/replay.sh from the feature worktree root and require eight queue cases, five ports, three controller edges, concurrency two, one activation per lease, zero crossed session values, zero forbidden surfaces, eight hashes, and a blocked gate.

confidence_probability_update.reason: V2 preserves the eight behavior cases and repairs both rejected proof paths: the recorded command is repository-root exact, and the forbidden-surface checker scans a modeled artifact that does not contain its fixture strings.

questions_answered: The exact recorded replay command resolves from the feature worktree root.

Zero messages, one delivery, burst above concurrency, handler failure, acknowledgement failure, lease loss and retry, shutdown while active, and two sessions in one server scope replay as eight distinct cases.
The burst reaches concurrency two, applies backpressure, and leaves zero active promises after join.
Every received lease has one activation execution and one acknowledge or reject port call.
Two sessions sharing one Scope keep session, issue, lease, observation, and result ownership values separate.
Receive, acknowledge, reject, lease validation, and wait are five required ports; watch, delivery, and triage are connected by three declared controller edges.
The forbidden-surface checker scans modeled-surface.json only, so its start, spawn, pool, and WorkerRegistry fixture names cannot self-match.
The modeled surface has no public lifecycle method and no hidden effect edge.
Ordinary graceful shutdown joins awaited promises. Forced ExecutionContext close remains conditional on accepted DKR-2 discovery and later Lite implementation.
questions_unanswered: A separate validator has not replayed the v2 behavior or surface checker.
DKR-2 forced-close discovery is not accepted and no queue implementation may depend on it today.
Provider-specific queue acknowledgement idempotency, lease fencing, receive shutdown, and distributed fairness remain physical adapter concerns.
decision: candidate only; bounded controller composition is preserved, root replay and fixture-excluding surface proof are repaired, and acceptance remains pending an independent v2 validator
flag_if_missing_or_stale: cannot: fail closed when the root replay, any behavior case, modeled surface, evidence hash, DKR-2 status, or independent review changes

No public lifecycle or pool surface is added.

No external service or product source was touched.
